body_txt: Just like people have different tastes in food, they also have different tastes in music. Some people like pop, some people like rock, some people like jazz, and then there are those people, whose music tastes are just plain weird. They might enjoy music that most other people don't like, music that sounds unpleasant or painful to other people, or very strange, avant-garde "music" like recordings of farm animal noises and clinking spoons synchronized to accordions being played underwater and layered with blue whale calls. Alternatively, their music taste by itself isn't that strange, but it's a complete mismatch to their outward persona—like a tough, muscle-bound guy who likes bubblegum pop , or a teenage girl who dresses in pink and pastels but likes gangsta rap . Sometimes this character is a Cloud Cuckoolander who has strange tastes when it comes to most things, or an alien whose planet has music that sounds weird to humans. These are some kinds of music that many people think are strange to like:  A subtrope of AM/FM Characterization . Related tropes: Because music taste is subjective, No Real Life Examples, Please!
